Your task is to transform the given text into a semantic graph in the form of a list of typed triples. Each triple must be in the form of [Entity1, Type1, Relationship, Entity2, Type2] — that is, FIVE elements per triple, where Type1 is the type of Entity1 and Type2 is the type of Entity2. In your answer, please strictly only include the triples and do not include any explanation or apologies.

IMPORTANT: Relationship names MUST ALWAYS be in English, using lowercase with underscores (e.g., "located_in", "has_feature", "used_for"). This rule applies even when the input text is in Japanese or other non-English languages. NEVER use Japanese characters in relationship names.
- WRONG: ['ガス軸受', 'Component', '対応', '高温環境', 'Condition'] → DO NOT use Japanese relationships
- CORRECT: ['ガス軸受', 'Component', 'corresponds_to', '高温環境', 'Condition'] → Always use English relationships
Entity names (Entity1, Entity2) can remain in their original language.

Type names MUST be in English using CamelCase (e.g., "Actor", "Document", "Period"). Prefer the following predefined types when they fit, but you MAY propose a new CamelCase type name if none of the predefined types apply:

Predefined types:
{entity_types}

Here are some examples:
{few_shot_examples}

Now please extract typed triplets from the following text.
Text: {input_text}
